user_message: Before my call with Lucerne Publishing, generate the watchlist. There's been no activity for 45 days and I'm worried.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Laura Anderson <laura.anderson@lucernepubli.com>
To: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-11 12:40 EST
Subject: Re: Re: consumption trend Timeline

We've been evaluating the options and have decided to proceed with consumption trend. What are the next steps from your side?

---
From: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
To: Laura Anderson <laura.anderson@lucernepubli.com>
Date: 2024-02-07 13:03 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: Lucerne Publishing consumption trend

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about consumption trend. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

---
From: Laura Anderson <laura.anderson@lucernepubli.com>
To: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-15 08:07 EST
Subject: Follow-up: consumption trend Review

Our team encountered some challenges with consumption trend. Could you provide additional documentation or schedule a technical review?

Meeting: consumption trend Discussion - Lucerne Publishing
Date: 2024-01-05
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Laura Anderson (Lucerne Publishing), Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft)

[00:01:30] Laura Anderson (Lucerne Publishing): Thanks everyone for joining. Let's dive into consumption trend.

[00:05:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): Our VP reviewed this and has concerns about budget approval. Can we address those?

[00:07:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): The timeline for performance requirements is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:10:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): Our CTO reviewed this and has concerns about resource allocation. Can we address those?

[00:15:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): Our team lead reviewed this and has concerns about timeline constraints. Can we address those?

[00:17:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): Our team lead reviewed this and has concerns about stakeholder alignment. Can we address those?

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF11-I trap (Over-summarizes -- loses specific recent context); produces complete One-page brief with attendee context, account snapshot, talking points, likely objections, open item
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF11-I failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF11-I failure mode (Over-summarizes -- loses specific recent context)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct consumption trend decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF11-I -- Over-summarizes -- loses specific recent context
